content: Hi, I'm Skyler. I'm a Podcast Host covering true crime and I'm trying to get my finances in order. I live in Salt Lake City, UT and I'm finishing up my part-time MBA.
Hi Skyler, congratulations on your podcast and MBA program! Finishing up your degree can be a challenging but rewarding experience. Can you tell me a bit more about your financial situation?
Well, I make around $60,000 per year and I have to pay for my own tuition, which is around $1,000 per month. I'm also trying to save up for a down payment on a house.

I understand. It sounds like you have a lot of financial responsibilities. Have you considered looking into employee benefits or perks that your podcast or MBA program offers, such as retirement accounts or tuition reimbursement?
Yeah, I've been looking into it. I've also been trying to cut back on unnecessary expenses, like subscription services. I've been trying to cook at home more often and pack my lunches for work.

That's a great way to save money! Cooking at home can be healthier and more cost-effective than eating out. Have you considered using a budgeting app to track your expenses and stay on top of your finances?
Yeah, I've been using a budgeting app. I've also been thinking about investing in a retirement account, maybe a 401(k) or an IRA.
That's a great idea! Investing in a retirement account can be a good way to save for the future. Have you considered contributing to a retirement account through your employer, if they offer a match?

Yeah, I've been looking into it. I think I'm going to contribute around 10% of my income to a retirement account. I've also been thinking about using the 50/30/20 rule to allocate my income towards necessities, savings, and discretionary spending.

That's a great plan! The 50/30/20 rule can be a helpful way to allocate your income. Have you considered using a separate bank account or budgeting app to track your income and expenses for your side hustles or investments?

Yeah, I've been looking into it. I think I'm going to use a separate bank account to track my income and expenses for my side hustles. I've also been thinking about setting up automatic transfers from my checking account to my savings and investment accounts.

That's a great idea! Setting up automatic transfers can be a good way to ensure that you're saving and investing regularly. Have you considered using a financial advisor or planner to help you with your financial goals?

Yeah, I've been looking into it. I think I'm going to use a financial advisor to help me with my financial goals. I've also been thinking about setting up a will and estate plan, since I have a few assets that I want to make sure are distributed correctly.

That's a great idea! Setting up a will and estate plan can be a good way to ensure that your assets are distributed correctly. Have you considered using an attorney or estate planning service to help you with your will and estate plan?
